texto: It’s not magic. I remember because I make comparisons. Not in terms of better or worse, just different. And not all of these memories are great, but they’re mine.Which lends way to believe, that none of our lives are put together on an assembly line. We’re not pre-packaged with memories or programmed with stories. We have to make our own.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Shane L. Koyczan sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “It’s not magic. I remember because I make comparisons. Not in terms of better or worse, just diff…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a motivacao, inspiracional, memory, útil em momentos de inspiracao e termino.

texto: Someone once told me that the finer points of devotion are about the size of a pinhole, and there are millions of them. And if you could connect each dot, then you’ve got a diagram of what you think you thought you knew, and if you’re willing to admit that you know nothing…you have the blueprint for a breakthrough.

texto: She tells me about dreams. She says my dreams are helium and balloons, and I've made the mistake of letting go a few to many times, but I still got this one. Tied around my finger like a wedding ring because even though I don't believe in marriages, I'm gonna bring this one home.

texto: I’m not the only kid who grew up this way. Surrounded by people who used to say that rhyme about sticks and stones. As if broken bones hurt more than the names we got called, and we got called them all. So we grew up believing no one would ever fall in love with us. That we’d be lonely forever. That we’d never meet someone to make us feel like the sun was something they built for us in their tool shed. So broken heart strings bled the blues as we tried to empty ourselves so we would feel nothing. Don’t tell me that hurts less than a broken bone.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Shane L. Koyczan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I’m not the only kid who grew up this way.
